title: Luxury resale marketplace lifts 2026 outlook after sales volume jumps 24%
text: The RealReal announced Q1 2026 results with GMV jumping 24% year-over-year to $606 million. The company lifted its full-year 2026 outlook based on the strong performance, now projecting GMV growth of 14%-16% for the full year. Revenue grew 19% to $190 million, exceeding analyst expectations of $188 million. The company achieved record active buyer growth of 10% year-over-year, reaching 1.083 million trailing twelve-month active buyers. Average order value surged 15% to $646, indicating strong demand for premium luxury categories. Adjusted EBITDA expanded significantly to $13.1 million (6.9% margin) from $4.1 million (2.6% margin) in Q1 2025, a 430 basis-point expansion. Management cited Athena AI system deployment and operational improvements as key drivers of the margin expansion. Direct revenue channel showed continued strength with premium direct-to-consumer growth. The company's supply-led growth strategy, supported by retail store expansion and partnership programs, is driving platform growth across all major categories. Management expressed confidence in sustaining double-digit growth rates while achieving further margin expansion through operational leverage and automation investments.
